days.description: Welcome the user. Ask for a temperature in Celsius. Convert it to Fahrenheit. Display the result. Skills: main(), variables, user input, return values, function parameters, basic arithmetic.
requirements: Ask for a temperature in Celsius

days.description: Ask for width and height. Calculate area and perimeter. Display both neatly. Extra: Add decimal support.
Input price, quantity, tax percentage. Calculate subtotal, tax amount, total price. Extra: Round to two decimal places.

days.concept: Multiple small functions, if/else, operator handling
days.description: Input number, operator, number. Supported operators: +, -, *, /. Display result. Inside calculate() use if/else. Extra: Handle division by zero.

days.concept: Header files, multiple source files, project organization
days.description: Split calculator into main.cpp, calculator.cpp, calculator.h. main() should only call functions. calculator.h contains declarations, calculator.cpp contains definitions.

days.concept: Syntax, runtime, logical errors, debugging workflow
days.description: Take your calculator. Intentionally create: syntax error, logical error, runtime error, incorrect output, missing return. Fix every one. Keep notes explaining why it happened and how you fixed it.

days.concept: Data types: integers, floating point, bool, char
days.description: Input weight (kg) and height (m). Calculate BMI. Display category: Underweight, Healthy, Overweight, Obese.
Ask for username. Rules: minimum length, maximum length, first letter alphabetic, no spaces. Display every failed rule.
Ask for age. Determine ticket price: child/adult/senior pricing. Bonus: Student discount.
requirements: Determine ticket price based on age

days.description: Input: Rock, Paper, Scissors. Computer choice is hardcoded. Determine winner.
Computer stores 42. Keep asking until guessed. Display: Too high, Too low, Correct. Count guesses.
requirements: Display Too high / Too low / Correct

days.description: Input a number (e.g., 7). Output multiplication table up to 12. Bonus: Let user choose ending number.
Print triangle patterns: left-aligned, right-aligned, pyramid. Uses nested loops.
For numbers 1–100: Fizz for multiples of 3, Buzz for 5, FizzBuzz for both, number otherwise. Bonus: User chooses ending number.
requirements: Print FizzBuzz for multiples of both
days.extraChallenge: Allow user to choose ending number

days.description: Roll two dice 100 times. Display every roll, total, average, highest roll, lowest roll.
requirements: Calculate and display total, average, highest, lowest

days.description: Create add(), multiply(), print() with multiple versions: add(int,int), add(double,double), add(int,int,int), etc.
Input 10 numbers. Display largest, smallest, average, sum. Bonus: Count even numbers.
Store 10 values. Reverse them manually without std::reverse().
Input a and b. Swap using pointers. Print before and after. No return values.
requirements: No return values from swap function

days.concept: Pointer arithmetic, array traversal without indexing
days.description: Store an array. Traverse using only pointer arithmetic. Find largest, smallest, average. No array[i] inside traversal function.
requirements: No array indexing in traversal function

days.concept: References vs pointers, when to use each
days.description: Rewrite Day 19 swap using references. Write comparison: when to choose pointer vs reference.
Each student has: name, age, grade. Allow user to enter 5 students, then display all records in a formatted table.
Create menu with numbered items (using enum). Let user choose items, calculate total bill, print itemized receipt.
Take Student Record or Restaurant system and reorganize: move reusable functions to separate files, eliminate unnecessary globals, keep variables in smallest possible scope.
Evaluate password: minimum length, uppercase, lowercase, number, special character. Display which requirements passed and overall strength rating.
Allow user to enter unknown number of grades (until they stop). Display: all grades, highest, lowest, average.
Extend Gradebook: search for a grade, sort ascending, sort descending, remove a grade. Use standard algorithms where appropriate.
Implement BankAccount class: account holder name, balance, deposit, withdraw, display balance. Instantiate multiple accounts in main().

days.concept: Constructors, private members, getters, validation, vector of objects
days.description: Improve Bank Account: constructors, private data members, getter functions, input validation (no negative deposits), multiple accounts in std::vector.

days.concept: Comprehensive review: menus, functions, vectors, classes, pointers/references, input validation, multi-file organization
days.description: Menu-driven system: Add student, Remove student, Search by name, Display all students, Display class statistics (highest, lowest, average), Exit. Multi-file, clean organization, validated input.
days.extraChallenge: Organize across multiple .cpp/.h files; validate all input
